purpose: Critical hydraulic component supplying pressurized water to both boilers in the coffee machine

states.name: pump_efficiency
states.description: Pump efficiency degrading over time due to wear and fouling

states.name: water_flow_left
states.description: Water flow pressure to left boiler

states.name: water_flow_right
states.description: Water flow pressure to right boiler

parameters.name: pump_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for pump efficiency degradation

parameters.name: pump_usage_factor
parameters.description: Usage intensity factor affecting degradation rate

parameters.name: nominal_pressure
parameters.description: Nominal water supply pressure at full efficiency

parameters.name: flow_tracking_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for flow tracking

parameters.name: flow_split_ratio
parameters.description: Ratio of flow distribution between left and right

parameters.name: low_flow_threshold
parameters.description: Threshold below which flow is considered too low

state_transition_logic: pump_efficiency decreases by (pump_efficiency / pump_degradation_timescale) * pump_usage_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 100.0]
water_flow_left approaches (water_supply * flow_split_ratio) using first-order lag with coefficient flow_tracking_coefficient, clamped to [0.0, 5.0]
water_flow_right approaches (water_supply * (1 - flow_split_ratio)) using first-order lag with coefficient flow_tracking_coefficient, clamped to [0.0, 5.0]
assumptions: Pump degradation follows exponential decay with constant usage factor
Water flow splits equally between left and right boilers by default
Flow tracking represents hydraulic response delays in the system
Pump efficiency directly scales output pressure linearly
